item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: Library of Congress
License: Public Domain
Title: Just before the battle. Washington D.C., July 12. Senator Joseph C. O'Mahoney, […]ing, gets a haircut and shave today in the barbershop before going on the Senate floor […] the Administration Court Bill. […] Leader Joseph T. Robinson, O'Mahoney charged responsibility for delay rests on the desk of […] from Arkansas and no place else, 7/12/37
Author: Harris & Ewing
Release year: 1937

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Alan C licenses all of their photos under an All Rights Reserved license. As such, this is not compliant with CC BY-SA 3.0 and has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: forget it. should have read it to the end.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Man, I could really use a haircut. In all seriousness we have a few spots here in which in-line links can be added to the wider SCP universe, namely links to the MCD Hub, Mr. Dark's PoI file, and Assistance for the Boss Lady where Isabel Wondertainment makes her first major appearance. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-2201 was first discovered on 12/02/1882, during a raid of a Marshall Carter and Dark warehouse. SCP-2201 was promised as an item that would give “Perfect grooming for the esteemed gentlemen.”

To this:

SCP-2201 was first discovered on 12/02/1882, during a raid of a Marshall, Carter and Dark warehouse. SCP-2201 was promised as an item that would give “Perfect grooming for the esteemed gentlemen.”

Changing this:

SCP-2201-2: [Sighs] Once, yes. There used to be lots of barbers here, doing all sorts of work. Now it’s just me, catering to those who can still find the coins. Aside from Signor Dark, Signora Wondertainment, and some of the others, there aren't many regulars from where you are.

To this:

SCP-2201-2: [Sighs] Once, yes. There used to be lots of barbers here, doing all sorts of work. Now it’s just me, catering to those who can still find the coins. Aside from Signor Dark, Signora Wondertainment, and some of the others, there aren't many regulars from where you are.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The random SCP button never fails to disappoint me. This was certainly an enjoyable read. I am always a sucker for GOI tie-ins, so take my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: Upon rereading, I see no problems. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dislike the Wondertainment bit because I prefer that it be eternally uncertain whether Wondertainment is a person, organization, or something else. I realize, however, that it is a matter of personal preference. Everything else about this is magnificent, so happily upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty cool and a smooth read. The parts about the aliens didn't trip in my mind. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Frankly I think this is charming as hell. Weird and charming. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't like it for a few reasons.

  • The reference to Wondertainment feels like it's shoehorned in. It doesn't add anything to the article except to make it look like you're trying to make it more important than it is.
  • There's too much characterization. 2201-2 is given more personal attention than feels necessary. Put another way, it feels like a shoddy attempt to make a character everyone on the wiki knows about.
  • Calling it cross-dimensional just doesn't work. Certainly the building itself could originate from extra-dimensional space, but when 2201-2 makes repeated mentions to grooming tentacles it comes off as though you're trying to make it as abnormal as you can while still maintaining the Friendly Town Barbershop motif.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm just glad his name isn't Sweeney …

Love the bits that imply aliens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I disagree. While it does appear to be a more limited Mr. Deeds on the surface, it has some distinct differences. SCP-662 seems to be a unique object, while there appears to be multiple copies of this coin (Mr. Dark and Ms. Wondertainment seem to still be using Giuseppe's services). Other worlds seem to be involved as well (I mean, my God, have you seen the colors kids are dyeing their tentacles recently!). I think it's a shame that he doesn't get paid for his services, or at least tipped.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After consulting with Roget, I have removed the mainpage attribution, and left it to the comments section only.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. It's kind of an inverse Mr. Deeds (you go to him instead) who is a barber. Helpful skips are nice when they aren't too helpful.

I will chime in on the above comments about the image attribution, though, if only to say it doesn't sound like "Agent" Alan C. could have actually taken that picture, since the article implies that only one person at a time can go to 2201-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to have to agree, but for different reasons. Attribution in the comment thread is fine by me, and doing it on-page is as immersion-breaking as having Wikipedia links.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't upvote an SCP that links to a flickr account in an image caption. I get that you want to attribute the photo properly but honestly if that's the only way you can do it, it'd be best to ditch the photo entirely or find another one. This is supposed to be the official writeup of an anomaly so dangerous that it has to be contained by a shadowy world spanning secret organisation, and you've got a link saying "Agent Alan C also takes some pretty boss pictures of trains, scope them out if you've got a minute bro".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to feel more than "eh" to endorse an article's survival on the wiki, and this isn't really inspiring much more than "eh". It's also weighed down by shoehorned GoI references.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think what he meant was:

SCP-2201-1 is a designation referring to an anomalous building

Should just be:

SCP-2201-1 is an anomalous building

Similar to:

SCP-2201 is a tin coin

If not, then I still think it sounds better that way…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In retrospect, it makes more sense to flip it. Doing so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The door doesn't open, and 2201-2 gets agitated whenever people try to. Also, given that this is closer to an active field exploration, as opposed to a controlled experiment, it makes more sense to send in an agent to conduct an interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The link is to the original owner's flickr page, where the image was first used. I'm just attributing it here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2201 is a designation referring to an anomalous building resembling a 1930’s barbershop.

So the designation is what's being contained?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Signora Wondertainment? Does that mean she is married?

E: Plus, unless done on purpose, "il Barberia" isn't correct. It should be "la Barberia".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why are they sending in an Agent, rather than a Researcher? Why doesn't he just walk out the door to find out where it is?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty cool, I'm okay with it on a level. Seems a little bit two-dimensional and the link on the picture is… unorthodox. I'm not sure why you'd link that. All in all, not to shabby. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isabel has sorta become the go-to Wondertainment ever since Djoric's tale. Look around. She's in just about everything recent.

E: Ninja Weizhong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Which was that?

I had not read that. Glad I did.

Damn, I hate being the one who appears to assume you've got the same reading history as me. Rookie mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Signora Wondertainment is more of a reference to Djoric's tale arc in which she originally appeared.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty blunt, and I think the idea could be executed better. And is the bit about Signora Wondertainment an allusion to the Cool War story arc?

Or just a thing?

And, I don't know what the updated standards for image attribution are, but the way it's linked in the subheading of the photo in the article is without elegance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn't seem to be anything but SCP-662 with a more limited skill set, gotta downvote.

item.title: Author Overview
item.content:encoded:
This was written up pretty much on a random whim. The image originally comes from here. The image is used here, but expressly belongs to Alan C. I just followed prior standards in attributing this image.

Thanks to the folks in chat for taking a look at it.
